functions.name: addBitwardenSecretManager
functions.description: Adds a Bitwarden Secrets Manager resource. The `projectNameOrId` parameter resolves to either a project name (creates or finds by name) or a project identifier GUID (adopts the existing project by ID).
functions.signature: addBitwardenSecretManager(name: string, projectNameOrId: ParameterResource, organizationId: ParameterResource, accessToken: ParameterResource): BitwardenSecretManagerResource

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The resource name.

parameters.name: projectNameOrId
parameters.description: The parameter that resolves to the Bitwarden project name or project identifier (GUID).

parameters.name: organizationId
parameters.description: The parameter that resolves to the Bitwarden organization identifier.

parameters.name: accessToken
parameters.description: The access token parameter used to manage the Bitwarden project and managed secrets.

functions.name: addSecret
functions.description: Adds a managed Bitwarden secret whose local and remote names are the same. The secret value is resolved from configuration key `Parameters:{parentName}-{name}`.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The Aspire resource name and Bitwarden secret name.

functions.name: addSecretWithRemoteName
functions.description: Adds a managed Bitwarden secret with distinct Aspire and remote names. The secret value is resolved from configuration key `Parameters:{parentName}-{name}`.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The Aspire resource name.

parameters.name: remoteName
parameters.description: The Bitwarden secret name.

functions.name: asSecretId
functions.description: Returns an `IExpressionValue` that resolves to the Bitwarden secret identifier. Pass it to `WithEnvironment` to inject the secret ID as an environment variable. The app then uses the Bitwarden SDK to fetch the secret value at runtime.
functions.returns: An expression value that resolves to the Bitwarden secret identifier.

functions.name: getSecret
functions.description: Gets or creates a Bitwarden secret reference whose Aspire and remote names are the same. The secret must already exist in Bitwarden; use `AddSecret` if Aspire should own and write the secret value.
functions.returns: A resource builder for the secret reference.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The Aspire resource name and Bitwarden secret name.

functions.name: getSecretById
functions.description: Gets or creates a Bitwarden secret reference by secret identifier. Use this when multiple secrets share the same name and the identifier is the only unambiguous key. The secret must already exist in Bitwarden; use `AddSecret` if Aspire should own and write the secret value.
functions.returns: A resource builder for the secret reference.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The Aspire resource name.

parameters.name: secretId
parameters.description: The Bitwarden secret identifier.

functions.name: getSecretWithRemoteName
functions.description: Gets or creates a Bitwarden secret reference with distinct Aspire and remote names. The secret must already exist in Bitwarden; use `AddSecret` if Aspire should own and write the secret value.
functions.returns: A resource builder for the secret reference.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The Aspire resource name.

parameters.name: remoteName
parameters.description: The Bitwarden secret name.

functions.name: withApiUrl
functions.description: Overrides the Bitwarden API URL.

parameters.name: apiUrl
parameters.description: The absolute Bitwarden API URL.

functions.name: withApiUrlFromExternalService
functions.description: Overrides the Bitwarden API URL using an external service resource. The Bitwarden resource will wait for the external service before authenticating.

parameters.name: server
parameters.description: The external service whose URL is used as the Bitwarden API URL.

functions.name: withApiUrlFromParameter
functions.description: Overrides the Bitwarden API URL using a parameter.

parameters.name: apiUrl
parameters.description: The parameter that resolves to the absolute Bitwarden API URL.

functions.name: withAuthCacheDirectory
functions.description: Overrides the AppHost auth cache directory (Bitwarden SDK auth session used by the AppHost reconciler). Defaults to the Aspire store when not set. Override to reuse a cached auth session across CI runs. To configure the auth cache directory inside the deployed app, use `WithBitwardenAuthCacheDirectory``1`.

parameters.name: authCacheDirectory
parameters.description: The auth cache directory on the AppHost, relative to the Aspire store when not rooted.

functions.name: withBitwardenReferenceAccessToken
functions.description: Overrides the Bitwarden access token injected into the connection for `source`. By default the management token is used. Supply a least-privilege read-only token here.

parameters.name: source
parameters.description: The Bitwarden resource whose connection name to target.

parameters.name: accessToken
parameters.description: The access token parameter for this connection.

functions.name: withBitwardenReferenceAuthCacheDirectory
functions.description: Configures the directory where the Bitwarden SDK stores its auth cache inside the resource, for the connection associated with `source`.

parameters.name: source
parameters.description: The Bitwarden resource whose connection name to target.

parameters.name: authCacheDirectory
parameters.description: The directory path inside the app where the auth cache file is stored.

functions.name: withBitwardenReferenceAuthCacheDirectoryFromParameter
functions.description: Configures the directory where the Bitwarden SDK stores its auth cache inside the resource, using a parameter, for the connection associated with `source`.

parameters.name: source
parameters.description: The Bitwarden resource whose connection name to target.

parameters.name: authCacheDirectory
parameters.description: A parameter whose value is the directory path inside the app.

functions.name: withBitwardenReferenceAuthCacheVolume
functions.description: Mounts a named volume and configures the Bitwarden SDK to store its auth cache there, for the connection associated with `source`. Use this for container resources. For process resources or when the container path is already known, use `WithBitwardenAuthCacheDirectory``1` instead.
functions.signature: withBitwardenReferenceAuthCacheVolume(source: BitwardenSecretManagerResource, volumeName?: string, containerDirectory?: string): IResourceWithEnvironment

parameters.name: source
parameters.description: The Bitwarden resource whose connection name to target.

parameters.name: volumeName
parameters.description: The Docker volume name. Defaults to `{resourceName}-{connectionName}-bitwarden-auth` when `null`.

parameters.name: containerDirectory
parameters.description: The directory inside the container where the volume is mounted. Defaults to `/var/lib/bitwarden`.

functions.name: withBitwardenSecretManagerReference
functions.description: Injects structured Bitwarden client configuration into the destination resource.

parameters.name: source
parameters.description: The Bitwarden resource builder.

parameters.name: connectionName
parameters.description: The logical connection name. Defaults to the Bitwarden resource name.

functions.name: withCacheFile
functions.description: Overrides the AppHost cache file path (integration bookkeeping: Bitwarden project ID, secret ID mappings). Defaults to `.bitwarden/{resourceName}.{environment}.json` relative to the AppHost directory. Override to share the cache across multiple AppHost projects, or to store it in a CI cache directory.

parameters.name: cacheFile
parameters.description: The cache file path, relative to the AppHost directory when not rooted.

functions.name: withIdentityUrl
functions.description: Overrides the Bitwarden identity URL.

parameters.name: identityUrl
parameters.description: The absolute Bitwarden identity URL.

functions.name: withIdentityUrlFromExternalService
functions.description: Overrides the Bitwarden identity URL using an external service resource. The Bitwarden resource will wait for the external service before authenticating.

parameters.name: server
parameters.description: The external service whose URL is used as the Bitwarden identity URL.

functions.name: withIdentityUrlFromParameter
functions.description: Overrides the Bitwarden identity URL using a parameter.

parameters.name: identityUrl
parameters.description: The parameter that resolves to the absolute Bitwarden identity URL.

handleTypes.name: BitwardenSecretManagerResource
handleTypes.description: Represents a Bitwarden Secrets Manager project and secret graph.
